# Ryan Duffy - Building with AI

> Solo SRE building a personal AI infrastructure platform on K3s. Public homelab journey documenting RAG architecture, multi-agent orchestration, MCP bridges, autonomous SRE agents, and the patterns that emerge when you treat your own life and work as a platform.

## About the author

Ryan Duffy is an SRE specialist at BT Group (UK), building Rootweaver in evenings and weekends since late 2024. Rootweaver is a personal AI infrastructure platform combining production RAG, autonomous SRE triage, MCP-as-primary surface, and cross-device unified compute on K3s with prosumer hardware (RTX 4080). The blog documents architectural decisions, lessons from production homelab use, and the broader pattern of single-engineer end-to-end platform building enabled by AI orchestration.

## Series

- [Season 3: Platform Engineering in Public](https://blog.rduffy.uk/posts/from-side-project-to-platform-how-running-out-of-disk-space-led-to-rebranding-an-entire-platform/) — Rebranding from ConvoCanvas to Rootweaver, the disk-space crisis that triggered the platform rewrite, autonomous SRE agent design, streaming journals with Kafka + KEDA + vLLM, dual knowledge graphs (documents + code), and the production-grade hardening that came with it. Oct 2025 onwards.
- [Season 2: Validation Chronicles](https://blog.rduffy.uk/posts/season-2-episode-1-validation-chronicles/) — Multi-agent RAG implementation, parallel execution patterns, quality gates, Anthropic 100/100 alignment, LLM-as-judge verification. Spring–autumn 2025.
- [Season 1: ConvoCanvas Origin](https://blog.rduffy.uk/posts/season-1-episode-1-day-zero/) — Day Zero through Episode 8. The founding journey: realising AI could be the orchestration partner, building the first MVP, the AI Awakening moment, K3s crash and recovery, diagram automation, meta-reflection. Sept–Oct 2025.

## Recent posts

- [The Second Graph Comes Online: Activation, Communities, and an AI Reversal](https://blog.rduffy.uk/posts/the-second-graph-comes-online-activation-communities-and-an-ai-reversal/) — S3E5. Code as queryable graph (FalkorDB + PDG), spaCy + EntityRuler beats LLM-based NER, the reversal that taught the right shape. May 2026.
- [The Day Everything Got Sealed](https://blog.rduffy.uk/posts/the-day-everything-got-sealed/) — S3E3. MCP bridge exposed to the internet via Cloudflare tunnel, the indexer leak, sealed-secrets remediation. Feb 2026.
- [Streaming Journals: Kafka Meets LLMs](https://blog.rduffy.uk/posts/streaming-journals-kafka-meets-llms/) — S3E2. Why JSONL beat OTEL for journal data, Kafka + KEDA architecture, vLLM workers, E2A + LLM-as-judge pattern. Feb 2026.
- [From Side Project to Platform](https://blog.rduffy.uk/posts/from-side-project-to-platform-how-running-out-of-disk-space-led-to-rebranding-an-entire-platform/) — S3E1. The 3.5-month silence, the disk-space crisis, the rebrand to Rootweaver, the 450 commits that followed. Feb 2026.

## Site resources

- [Sitemap (XML)](https://blog.rduffy.uk/sitemap-index.xml): all posts and pages
- [RSS feed](https://blog.rduffy.uk/rss.xml): subscribe to new episodes
- [About](https://blog.rduffy.uk/about/): author background and current focus

## Topics covered

- Personal AI infrastructure on K3s with prosumer GPU
- MCP (Model Context Protocol) — 29-tool aggregator pattern, agent dispatcher inside the bridge
- Multi-agent RAG — Fast/Deep/Oracle dispatcher with intent-based routing
- Vector + BM25 hybrid search via Qdrant with cross-encoder reranking
- FalkorDB + Graphiti + Program Dependency Graph for code intelligence
- Autonomous SRE agent — Alertmanager events via Kafka, LLM-backed triage with confidence scoring, 5-way evidence fan-out (Discord, Jira, Markdown vault, Postgres, Kafka)
- GitOps with FluxCD, sealed-secrets, observability with Prometheus + Grafana + Tempo + Pyroscope
- AI orchestration as a primary engineering mode — directing Claude Code / Aider / Perplexity as a virtual team rather than writing all code by hand
- Honest architectural decision records (ADRs) with reasoning + trade-offs
- Mac MLX as opt-in embedding accelerator for Apple Silicon

## Permission

Content on this blog is licensed for use in **AI search indexes** and **real-time AI grounding (RAG)**, but **not for AI model training**. See [robots.txt](https://blog.rduffy.uk/robots.txt) for the machine-readable Content-Signal declarations (`search=yes, ai-train=no`). Attribution to "Ryan Duffy / blog.rduffy.uk" is appreciated when citing.
